name: Jigsaw Trading
description_short: Jigsaw daytradr — a professional-grade, standalone order flow, DOM and trade execution platform built for serious futures and equities day traders. Connects directly to CQG, Rithmic, StoneX, Tradovate, Interactive Brokers and IQFeed. Includes charts, Reconstructed Tape, Auction Vista, Trade Simulator, Pace of Tape, Journalytix analytics, and daily Market Intelligence briefings. Not a casual-trader tool — this is a premium product at a premium price, built for people who trade size and make decisions from the order book.
description_long:
## Who This Is For — And Who It Isn't

Up front: **Jigsaw is a professional-grade tool, not a beginner platform.** Lifetime licences start at $579 and go up to $1,979, plus a $50/month or $500/year subscription if you want to trade real money. On top of the price tag, the tools assume you already understand order flow, auction theory, and tape reading — there is no "learn as you go" shortcut.

If you're a casual or part-time trader, a swing/position trader, or someone still learning the basics of the market, this is not the right tool. You'd be over-paying for capability you can't yet use.

If you're a serious futures or equities day trader — prop-firm, retail pro, or full-time discretionary scalper — and you make money (or want to) by reading the order book and the tape, this is one of the best-regarded tools in the category.

## What Jigsaw Trading Is

Jigsaw Trading's flagship product is **daytradr** — a standalone, multi-threaded order flow, depth-of-market and trade execution platform built for professional day traders. It connects directly to CQG, Rithmic, StoneX, Tradovate, Interactive Brokers, and IQFeed, which means a futures or equities trader can run their entire workflow — market data, charts, DOM, order entry, journaling, performance analytics — inside Jigsaw without needing NinjaTrader, Sierra Chart, or any other host platform.

For traders who already live inside NinjaTrader 7/8, MetaTrader 5, or MultiCharts.NET, Jigsaw also offers the **Platform Bridge** — a separate integration path that lets you layer Jigsaw's tools on top of your existing setup. But that's the secondary use case, not the main product.

This is a niche, pro-focused platform. If you don't know what absorption, initiative activity, or auction exhaustion mean, this is not the first tool you should buy. But if reading the tape and the DOM is central to how you trade, Jigsaw is one of the deepest implementations available anywhere.

## The Company

Jigsaw Trading was founded by **Peter Davies**, a UK-based professional trader with decades of experience in futures order flow trading. Davies built Jigsaw out of frustration with the DOM tools available in mainstream platforms — he wanted something that specifically focused on making tape reading more efficient and quantifiable. The company is small and focused, and Davies still appears in live market sessions, YouTube content, and customer support regularly.

Because Davies is an active trader, Jigsaw ships with both the software and an opinionated methodology for how to actually use it. That combination — tool plus framework — is a large part of why long-time Jigsaw customers tend to stay.

## daytradr — The Flagship Platform

daytradr is a full trading platform, not an add-on. The core components:

- **Depth & Sales (DOM / Price Ladder)** — the signature order-entry window. Shows resting orders, executions, trader positioning / stop locations, order cancellations, stacking and iceberging, trade balance, order queue position, and per-price P&L. One-click trading with configurable brackets and auto-exit strategies.
- **Reconstructed Tape** — filters out insignificant trades, aggregates rapid-fire executions at the same price, color-codes by aggressor, highlights unusually large trades. The feature most Jigsaw users cite as the single most valuable tool.
- **Summary Tape** — aggregates all trading activity at each price level: total volume, buy/sell delta, imbalance, and running comparison across levels.
- **Auction Vista** — a heatmap-style chart that visualises how the market auction unfolds: liquidity shading, Large Trade Circles from Jigsaw's proprietary algorithms, absorption patterns, and direct trade management straight off the chart.
- **LTF Charts** — a full "out of the box" charting package designed for lower-timeframe active traders.
- **Trade Simulator** — realistic fills on limit orders and realistic slippage on market and stop orders, using daytradr's own DOM. Ideal for learning, testing strategies, and prop-firm evaluation practice.
- **Smart Gauges** — including the **Pace of Tape (PoT)** gauge (over 50 styles) measuring trading pace vs. recent historical average, plus market-depth meters, order-book pulling/stacking indicators, and trade-filtered meters.
- **Order Flow Event Alerts** — iceberg alerts, block trade alerts, large trade alerts, and divergence alerts.
- **Execution tools** — one-click trading, auto order types, volume-based stops, automated exit strategies, exchange-traded spread support, custom sessions, and workspace management.

## Charts Included

daytradr includes a full charting package — it is not purely a DOM tool:

- Candlestick and industry-standard chart types
- **JT Footprint** charts
- **JT Reversal Footprint** charts
- **JT Market Profile** (traditional market profile)
- **Auction Vista** heatmap
- Price Delta, Cumulative Delta, Volume Delta Bars, Depth Histogram, Flip Charts
- **JT CSI** (Cumulative Sheep Index) and other custom indicators

Full list on Jigsaw's own documentation at daytradr.jigsawtrading.com/charts.html.

## Trade Journal & Performance Analytics (Journalytix)

Included with every Jigsaw lifetime bundle — not a paid add-on. Journalytix is Jigsaw's web-based trade journal that captures order and position data within seconds of execution, and provides:

- Profitability breakdowns by time of day, day of week, instrument and account
- Monthly / weekly / daily performance calendars with trade count, P&L, win rate, max drawdown and max gain
- 30-minute interval performance tracking inside the trading day
- Combination-factor analysis to pinpoint what is actually driving wins and losses
- "What-if" analysis on historical trades
- Leaderboard with a consistency-rating algorithm modelled on prop-firm go-live standards

## Market Intelligence — Daily Pre-Market Briefings

Market Intelligence is a separate Jigsaw product: an AI-generated, pre-market briefing system for futures traders, delivered daily before the open. It covers ES, NQ, GC, CL, ZC, ZN and 6E (with more markets being added), and includes:

- Overnight action summary and session themes
- News items tagged with red/yellow/green severity and directional arrows
- Scheduled economic releases, Fed speakers, and earnings with expected-vs-actual comparisons
- Risk calendar timeline of market-moving events, filtered per instrument
- Session-by-session action plan for what to watch
- Key levels and flow data — price levels, COT positioning, ETF flows, institutional sentiment

Pricing: included with the daytradr Core 4Life bundle + live-trading subscription, or available standalone at $50/month or $500/year.

## Platform Bridge (Optional)

For traders who prefer to stay inside an existing ecosystem, the **Jigsaw Platform Bridge** integrates Jigsaw's tools with:

- **NinjaTrader 7 / 8**
- **MetaTrader 5**
- **MultiCharts.NET** (and MultiCharts.NET SE)

This is an alternative route, not a requirement. daytradr itself runs standalone with direct broker and data connections.

## Operating Systems & Markets

- **Windows** — native installation
- **Mac** — via Bootcamp or Parallels
- **Markets**: Futures and exchange-traded spreads (primary), plus NYSE stocks via Interactive Brokers TWS and IQFeed, and global equities across US, Europe, Hong Kong, Sydney and Osaka. Crypto and forex are not supported.

## Education & Methodology

Jigsaw invests heavily in education, and every lifetime bundle includes real training — not just software tutorials:

- **Jigsaw Trading YouTube** — extensive library of DOM methodology, live market analysis, and tool walkthroughs
- **Advanced Order Flow Training Pack** — included with every lifetime tier
- **Institutional training** — trading with price and order flow strategies
- **Customer-exclusive trading education** — a separate library for paid customers
- **Live chat room** — ongoing community and trade discussion
- **1-on-1 consultation chats** — included with every bundle
- **Regular webinars** — live sessions on order flow concepts and trade analysis

This matters because Jigsaw's tools are powerful but require genuine skill to use well. The education is a real differentiator, not a marketing afterthought.

## Pricing — Be Realistic About Total Cost

Jigsaw is not cheap and is not pretending to be. The pricing model is built for professionals, not casual traders, and the total cost of ownership is higher than the headline lifetime figure suggests.

**Three lifetime-licence tiers** — all three contain the **same daytradr software**, and differ only in the education and training included:

- **Independent** — $579 one-time purchase
- **Professional** — $879 one-time purchase
- **Institutional** — $1,979 one-time purchase

All lifetime tiers include: daytradr platform licence for 2 PCs for life, Journalytix trade analytics, Trade Simulator, live chat room, leaderboard access, free lifetime updates, and hands-on support.

**Live-trading subscription (required for real-money trading):** $50/month or $500/year. Covers live-trade licensing for 2 PCs, Market Intelligence daily briefings, real-time news and audio feeds, real-time economic calendar with pre-release warnings, advanced real-time analytics, real-time cloud journaling, and priority support.

**Realistic first-year cost for a live trader:** $579 (Independent lifetime) + $500 (annual live subscription) = **~$1,079 in year one**, then ~$500/year ongoing. Add broker data fees and exchange-data subscriptions on top of that, and that's before your trading capital. If $1,000+ in tooling costs for year one is a problem, this isn't the platform for you — go learn on something free first.

**SIM / demo / prop-firm evaluation trading:** free — no subscription required. Jigsaw's rule is "if it's not real money, it's free," which makes the platform unusually well-priced for traders still working through prop-firm evaluations before going live.

**Money-back guarantee:** 14 days on the software cost. This is a refund after purchase, not a free trial — the software is paid for up front and refunded on request within 14 days. Training is non-refundable.

## What Jigsaw Does Not Have

- **No automated / algorithmic trading** — entirely manual and discretionary
- **No crypto or forex** — futures and equities only
- **No mobile app** — desktop only (Windows native, Mac via Bootcamp / Parallels)

## Who Jigsaw Trading Is For

**Good fit:**
- Professional futures scalpers and full-time discretionary day traders who make decisions from the DOM and tape
- Prop-firm traders running evaluations — the free SIM + realistic-fill Trade Simulator + Journalytix consistency leaderboard map directly onto most prop firm go-live requirements
- Experienced retail pros already trading live, trading real size, and looking for better DOM and order-flow tooling than mainstream platforms ship with
- Futures and equities traders who want a full standalone professional platform — charts, DOM, execution, journal, analytics — without stitching together multiple vendors
- Traders already inside NinjaTrader, MetaTrader 5, or MultiCharts.NET who want to add Jigsaw's DOM and order flow tools on top via the Platform Bridge
- Traders who value a founder-led product with an opinionated methodology, not just generic software

**Not the right fit:**
- Beginners still learning trading basics — the learning curve is steep and assumes prior knowledge of order flow, auction theory, and tape reading
- Casual or part-time retail traders who can't justify $1,000+ in year-one tooling costs
- Swing and position traders — the platform is built for short-timeframe active trading, not multi-day positions
- Anyone trading crypto or forex
- Traders who want full automated or algorithmic execution

## Community Reception & Third-Party Reviews

We looked at how Jigsaw is received outside its own marketing:

- **Trustpilot:** 4.7 out of 5 ("Excellent") across ~34 customer reviews as of early 2026. Recurring themes: responsive support ("feels more like a family business"), a CEO who actually engages with user suggestions, and reports of serious prop-firm traders using the platform.
- **YouTube:** Jigsaw's channel has ~26,000 subscribers with years of DOM methodology walkthroughs, live market sessions, and tool tutorials — unusually deep educational library for an order-flow vendor.
- **John Grady (CEO, No BS DayTrading):** "I think it is the best platform hands-down — you just see certain things in it that you don't see in other platforms. It clearly shows volume ebb and flow. Customer service is excellent, any time you have a problem they respond very quickly."
- **Elite Trader forum history:** threads track real product development over the years — Trade Simulator release, Platform Bridge for NinjaTrader and MetaTrader 5, Journalytix launch, trailing stops + limits. This is a product that has been shipping continuously, not a static tool.
- **Fair-balance critical views:** not everyone is a fan. Some experienced forum users have described Jigsaw as a "one-man shop" and questioned institutional uptake. Heavier-size traders on the Tradovate bridge have flagged it as better suited to smaller position sizes. These are minority views and not universal, but they exist and are worth weighing.

## A Note on Outdated Pricing Claims

A lot of third-party review sites still quote Jigsaw pricing at "$197/month" or "$597 lifetime". Neither is correct and, per Jigsaw, the $197/month figure was never real. The actual current pricing is the three lifetime tiers ($579 / $879 / $1,979) plus the optional $50/month or $500/year live-trading subscription documented above. If you see a review quoting monthly software pricing, it's out of date.

## Bottom Line

Jigsaw daytradr is a full, standalone, **professional-grade** order flow, DOM, charting, execution and analytics platform — not a plug-in, not a beginner tool, and not cheap. The Reconstructed Tape, Summary Tape, Auction Vista, Pace of Tape, Trade Simulator, and Journalytix analytics are each strong tools in their own right, and the lifetime-licence pricing is unusually trader-friendly in a market dominated by recurring SaaS.

But the price tag, the live-data subscription, and the learning curve all add up, and this is not a platform you buy on a whim. If you're a casual or part-time trader, look elsewhere — you'll pay for capability you can't yet use. If you're a serious, full-time futures or equities day trader who makes decisions from the order book and the tape — and who wants an active-trader founder, a real support team, a 26,000-subscriber education library, and a platform that has been shipping continuously since 2011 behind your workflow — Jigsaw is a genuinely best-in-class option and is worth the investment.

pricing.free_plan_answer: No. Jigsaw Trading does not offer a free plan for live trading. The cheapest lifetime licence is $579 one-time (Independent), and real-money trading also requires a $50/month or $500/year live-trading subscription. Jigsaw does not offer a free trial — instead it offers a 14-day money-back guarantee on the software cost. SIM, demo and prop-firm evaluation trading is free for existing licence holders.
